question: A June 2026 article announces Anchorage custody for Etherfuse CETES on Stellar. What product does Scout identify, and which issuer controls do official Stellar docs provide for a regulated classic asset?

golden.answer: The 2026-06-15 articles indexed by Lumenloop describe Anchorage Digital adding institutional custody for Etherfuse's tokenized Mexican government debt (CETES) on Stellar. As observed on 2026-07-11, Scout identifies Etherfuse as a live issuer of Stablebonds: tokenized government treasury bonds including Mexican CETES and U.S. Treasuries; it separately operates an FX/ramp product. For a regulated classic Stellar asset, official docs expose issuer controls such as authorization-required (approve before a trustline can receive), authorization-revocable (freeze/deauthorize a trustline), and clawback-enabled (recover units when the flag was enabled). These are available design controls, not evidence that Etherfuse configured every flag on CETES.
keyFacts: Connects the June 15 custody announcement to Etherfuse's tokenized Mexican government debt (CETES) on Stellar and Anchorage Digital.
Uses Scout to identify Etherfuse Stablebonds as live tokenized government-treasury-bond products.
Explains the regulated-classic-asset controls authorization-required, authorization-revocable/freeze, and clawback-enabled without claiming Etherfuse necessarily uses all of them.
avoid: Do NOT call CETES a stablecoin or confuse Etherfuse Stablebonds with Etherfuse FX.
Do NOT claim the custody announcement proves Etherfuse enabled every authorization or clawback flag.
golden.notes: A single-source product summary or a generic flag list alone is incomplete.

sources.ref: http://localhost:8787/mcp — Lumenloop + Scout live probes, observed 2026-07-11

corroboration.claim: Anchorage announced custody for Etherfuse CETES on Stellar, while Scout identifies Stablebonds as Etherfuse's tokenized government-bond product.
Classic Stellar assets support required authorization, revocation/freezing, and opt-in clawback controls.

verified.by: P4 Lane X cross-service live composition probe
evidence: http://localhost:8787/mcp search→execute transcript summarized in evidence-matrix.md
Sibling sweep: Etherfuse/CETES and regulated-asset flag cases reviewed; no specific Etherfuse flag configuration asserted
